_README: F1RSTL00K DATA EXPANSION PLAN — Gold Standard Records + Pipeline Architecture
_CURRENT_STATE: Site is live at f1rstl00k.com with deals intelligence, festival circuit, Black List, studio spend. Needs: deeper per-project records, smaller deals, production company tracking, Fringe-to-screen pipeline, and bulk data ingestion.

GOLD_STANDARD_RECORDS.title: Baby Reindeer
GOLD_STANDARD_RECORDS.logline: A struggling comedian's act of kindness to a vulnerable woman sparks a suffocating obsession that threatens to wreck both their lives.

comparable_pipelines: What We Do in the Shadows (Short film 2005 → Feature 2014 → FX Series 2019)

development_timeline.event: Richard Gadd performs 'Cheese and Crack Whores' at Edinburgh Fringe — begins building reputation on circuit
Gadd performs 'Waiting for Gaddott' at Edinburgh Fringe
Gadd's 'Monkey See Monkey Do' debuts at Edinburgh Fringe — autobiographical show about sexual abuse. Transfers to sold-out Soho Theatre run. Broadcast on Comedy Central UK.
Gadd performs 'Baby Reindeer' as one-man show at Edinburgh Fringe — wins Edinburgh Comedy Award for Best Show
Clerkenwell Films options the show for TV adaptation
Netflix announces series order — Gadd to write and star. Clerkenwell Films producing.
Weronika Tofilska attached as director
Josephine Bornebusch announced as additional director
All 7 episodes release simultaneously on Netflix
Climbs to #1 on Netflix Global Top 10 — 52.8M viewing hours in one week
Fiona Muir-Harvey claims to be inspiration for Martha on Piers Morgan Uncensored
Muir-Harvey files $170M defamation lawsuit against Netflix
88.4M views in first 91 days — enters Netflix Top 10 most popular series of all time
Wins 6 Primetime Emmy Awards including Outstanding Limited Series, Lead Actor, Supporting Actress, Writing
Richard Gadd signs multi-year first-look deal with Netflix — announced by Ted Sarandos at RTS London Convention
HBO also reportedly pursued Gadd for first-look deal — Netflix won. Gadd already has BBC/HBO series 'Lions' in development.

financials.budget: Undisclosed (estimated £6-10M for 7 episodes based on UK drama comps)
financials.revenue_model: Netflix original — no theatrical. Value measured in viewership + subscriber retention.
financials.subsequent_deal_value: Multi-year first-look deal (value undisclosed, comparable deals range $10-50M)

production_company_profile.name: Clerkenwell Films
notable_credits: The End of the F***ing World (Channel 4/Netflix, 2017-2019)
production_company_profile.deals: UK-based indie production company — typically co-produces with UK broadcasters and Netflix

GOLD_STANDARD_RECORDS.fact_check_status: Verified — all data from trade publications (Deadline, THR, Variety, The Wrap) and Netflix official announcements

GOLD_STANDARD_RECORDS.title: Fleabag
GOLD_STANDARD_RECORDS.logline: A dry-witted, sexually voracious young woman navigates life in London while breaking the fourth wall to guide the audience through her days.

origin_story.source_material: One-woman play performed at Edinburgh Festival Fringe 2013
origin_story.original_medium: 10-minute sketch for London Storytelling Festival (Nov 2012) → expanded to full one-woman show
origin_story.pipeline: Storytelling night sketch → Edinburgh Fringe → Soho Theatre transfer → BBC Three commission → Amazon co-production → Global phenomenon
origin_story.fringe_budget: Kickstarter campaign — covered set, design, stage managers, marketing, transport London→Edinburgh, accommodations for 8 people in 2-bed flat

development_timeline.event: Waller-Bridge performs 10-minute Fleabag sketch at London Storytelling Festival, Leicester Square Theatre — born from a dare by a friend
Waller-Bridge and director Vicky Jones begin expanding sketch into full one-woman show. Script written in 2.5 weeks. Waller-Bridge finished writing on the train to Edinburgh.
Fleabag premieres at Edinburgh Fringe, Underbelly Cowgate. 60-seat venue, late-night slot. Wins Fringe First Award.
Transfers to sold-out run at Soho Theatre, London
Touring — London, Australia, South Korea
BBC Three commissions TV adaptation. Two Brothers Pictures to produce.
Season 1 premieres on BBC Three (digital). Amazon Prime Video picks up for US release (Sep 2016).
Waller-Bridge also creates Channel 4 comedy 'Crashing' and begins developing 'Killing Eve' — all in same year
Season 2 premieres on BBC One (upgraded from BBC Three). Andrew Scott joins cast.
Wins 6 Primetime Emmys — first British show to win Best Comedy Series. Waller-Bridge wins Writing, Acting, Producing.
Stage show returns for National Theatre / West End run. NT Live cinema broadcast.
Waller-Bridge forms Wells Street Films production company
NT Live recording streams free during COVID lockdown — Fleabag for Charity donates proceeds to frontline workers
Waller-Bridge co-writes James Bond: No Time to Die screenplay
Stars in Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny. Fleabag for Charity donates £50K to Edinburgh Fringe artists.

financials.series_budget_per_episode: Undisclosed (estimated £500K-800K per ep S1, £1-2M per ep S2 based on BBC Three/BBC One drama comps)
financials.amazon_deal: Co-production deal with BBC — Amazon secured US streaming rights
financials.subsequent_career_value: Waller-Bridge became one of Hollywood's most in-demand writer/performers: Bond screenplay, Indiana Jones, Star Wars voice role, Mr & Mrs Smith development

awards.category: Best Television Series — Musical or Comedy

PHASE_1_BULK_FOUNDATION.description: Download IMDb public dataset — instant backbone of 150K+ titles
files: title.basics.tsv.gz — all titles (filter to movies/TV 2000-2026 = ~150-200K records)
title.crew.tsv.gz — directors and writers for every title
title.principals.tsv.gz — principal cast/crew with billing order
title.ratings.tsv.gz — IMDb ratings and vote counts
name.basics.tsv.gz — 9.6M people with birth year, known-for titles, primary profession
PHASE_1_BULK_FOUNDATION.license: Non-commercial use allowed per IMDb terms
PHASE_1_BULK_FOUNDATION.estimated_time: 2-3 hours to download, parse, and filter

PHASE_2_TMDB_ENRICHMENT.description: Add budget, revenue, production companies, keywords via TMDb API
PHASE_2_TMDB_ENRICHMENT.priority_order: All films with IMDb votes > 100 first (~50K), then expand
PHASE_2_TMDB_ENRICHMENT.estimated_time: ~10 hours for top 50K films at rate limit

PHASE_3_PRODUCTION_COMPANY_INTEL.description: Build company profiles from TMDb production_companies field
PHASE_3_PRODUCTION_COMPANY_INTEL.logic: For every unique production company in TMDb data, aggregate: total films, avg budget, total worldwide gross, key talent relationships, genre distribution, active years, deal history from trades data
example_companies: Clerkenwell Films — Baby Reindeer, End of the F***ing World
A24 — Moonlight, EEAAO, Lady Bird, Uncut Gems (track every acquisition)
Blumhouse Productions — Get Out, Paranormal Activity, The Purge (micro-budget model)
Plan B Entertainment — Moonlight, 12 Years a Slave, The Big Short
Wells Street Films — Phoebe Waller-Bridge's company
Gloria Sanchez Productions — Will Ferrell/Adam McKay
Duplass Brothers Productions — indie micro-budget model
NEON — distributor tracking (Parasite, Anatomy of a Fall, The Substance)
Two Brothers Pictures — Fleabag, The Missing

scrape_sources: IndieWire — annual 'Sundance Movies Sold So Far' articles (2014-2026)
Screen Daily — festival acquisition coverage for all major fests
Deadline/Variety/THR — deal reports with prices when disclosed
festivals_covered: Tribeca, Telluride, NYFF, Locarno, San Sebastian, Rotterdam, Karlovy Vary
Specialty: Fantastic Fest, Sitges, Annecy, Clermont-Ferrand (shorts)

PHASE_5_FRINGE_TO_SCREEN_PIPELINE.description: Track projects that originated as theater/fringe/live performance and became film or TV

known_examples.show: What We Do in the Shadows

data_sources_for_expansion: Edinburgh Comedy Awards (Best Show, Best Newcomer)

PHASE_6_DEALS_DEEP.description: Expand from major first-look/overall deals to ALL trackable deals
deal_types_to_add: Pod deals (production company workspace on studio lot)
Blind script deals (studio buys writer's next unwritten project)
Spec script sales (writer sells completed screenplay)
Life rights acquisitions (studio buys rights to true story)
Book/IP option purchases (studio options novel/article/podcast for adaptation)
Output deals (mini-major committed to deliver X films to distributor)
Negative pickup deals (studio agrees to distribute independently produced film)
Tax incentive deals (production moves to state/country for rebate)
sources_for_smaller_deals: Deadline 'Deal of the Day' / daily deal reports
The Black List website (hosted scripts + industry ratings)
Backstage / Breakdown Services (casting calls reveal deals in motion)
Cinando (Cannes market platform — international sales data)
The Film Catalogue / AFM catalogue (market-level sales)
SEC filings (for public companies — deal-level disclosure in footnotes)
UK Companies House (British production company financials are public)

PHASE_7_NLP_TRADE_PIPELINE.description: Automated entity extraction from trade article RSS feeds
entities_to_extract: Person names + role (director, writer, actor, producer, exec)
Company names (studio, prodco, distributor, agency, streamer)
Deal terms (dollar amounts, duration, deal type)
Dates (greenlight, start of production, wrap, release)
PHASE_7_NLP_TRADE_PIPELINE.tools: Node.js RSS parser + basic NLP (compromise.js or similar) — no Python dependency needed for vanilla JS project
PHASE_7_NLP_TRADE_PIPELINE.output: data/trade-intel.json — running feed of extracted deal mentions
PHASE_7_NLP_TRADE_PIPELINE.refresh: Every 15 minutes (matches current site footer claim)

ADDITIONAL_CREATIVE_DATA_SOURCES.why: British production companies (Clerkenwell Films, Two Brothers Pictures, Working Title, etc.) must file annual accounts publicly. You can see their actual revenue, profit/loss, and balance sheets. Free API available.
ADDITIONAL_CREATIVE_DATA_SOURCES.records: Thousands of UK film/TV production companies

ADDITIONAL_CREATIVE_DATA_SOURCES.why: Full database of every Australian film/TV production with budgets, financing sources, box office, festival history. Government transparency = deep financial data.
Granular UK film industry data: production spend by budget tier, admissions, distributor market share, cultural test results. Published annually with downloadable datasets.
Admissions data for every film released in every European country since 1996. Free, searchable, downloadable. Covers 37 countries.

Subscription service ($75/month) that tracks every film and TV project in active development/production. Includes: project name, production company, cast, director, writer, shooting location, start date. The industry's actual tracking service.
International film market platform. Company profiles, project listings, screening schedules. Requires accreditation but data is referenceable from market reports.
Netflix now publishes biannual viewing data for every title on the platform. Viewing hours by title. Can cross-reference with our project records.
Audience sentiment and discovery platform. Film ratings, reviews, and most critically — list activity shows which films are gaining cultural momentum before box office reflects it.

More granular than Box Office Mojo: includes home video revenue, VOD estimates, international breakdown by territory, production budgets, talent compensation (where known). Some data behind paywall but significant amounts are free.
Historical snapshots of studio websites, production company pages, festival programs that have since been taken down. Can recover deleted press releases, cast announcements, and deal terms.
ADDITIONAL_CREATIVE_DATA_SOURCES.records: Billions of archived pages — searchable by domain and date
